# Version 4.151.0 (Unreleased)
> **Nano Server fonts and performance**

## Highlights

SkiaSharp 4.151.0 adds FreeType-backed font support on Windows Nano Server and ships targeted performance improvements. A long-standing gap — fonts on Nano Server — is closed with a community contribution enabling FreeType as the font backend. Alongside that, two hot-path allocations are eliminated and a memory leak in the raster image failure path is fixed.

## Breaking Changes

*None in this preview line.*

## Platform

- **Font rendering on Windows Nano Server** — Nano Server ships without the Windows GDI/DirectWrite font stack. SkiaSharp now uses FreeType as the font backend on that target so text rendering works out of the box. — ❤️ [@jeffska](https://github.com/jeffska) ([#4280](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4280))
- **Containerized test coverage expanded** — CI now validates SkiaSharp on Azure Linux, Alpine (musl libc), and Nano Server via dedicated container test legs, ensuring the library works correctly across the full range of supported Linux and Windows Server targets. ([#4515](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4515))

## Bug Fixes

- **Memory leak in SKImage raster creation fixed** — When the native raster image creation failed, the CoTaskMem pixel buffer allocated for the operation was not freed, causing a native memory leak. The failure path now correctly releases the buffer. ([#4455](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4455))
- **Hot-path allocations eliminated** — GetEncodedText no longer allocates an intermediate string on the null-terminated fast path, and SKRuntimeEffectUniforms now pre-sizes its internal lookup map to the known uniform count — both removing unnecessary allocations on frequently called code paths. ([#4498](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4498), [#4512](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4512))
